data.description: SENIOR PENTAGON OFFICIAL SAYS U.S. HAS SPENT $25 BILLION ON IRAN WAR SO FAR

data.title: Starbucks Leads Seven Stocks Flashing Buy Signals On Earnings
data.description: Shares of Starbucks are approaching an entry point following the release of their latest financial results. Alongside this, six other stocks ranging from data centers to semiconductor companies are also approaching buy points. Starbucks, in particular, is leading the pack with seven stocks showing promising buy signals after strong earnings reports. This information was confirmed by an article from Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Silicon Motion Stock Rockets As Sales More Than Double
data.description: Silicon Motion Technology exceeded expectations in Q1 with its financial results and Q2 outlook. The stock of SIMO experienced a significant surge on Wednesday following this news. The post highlighted how sales more than doubled, showcasing a strong performance by the company.

data.title: Regeneron Slumps On An Unexpected Setback For Its Bread-And-Butter Drug
data.description: Regeneron experienced a decline in stock value on Wednesday morning following lower-than-anticipated U.S. sales of its high-dose Eylea, which fell short of Wall Street's projections. This unexpected setback for the company's key drug impacted its performance, leading to a drop in stock prices. The specific figures regarding the sales performance and the extent of the stock decline were not provided, but it is evident that Regeneron faced challenges in meeting market expectations with its crucial product Eylea.

data.title: T-Mobile Shoots Down Cable TV Acquisition On Q1 Earnings Call
data.description: Following the announcement during their Q1 earnings call, T-Mobile's stock saw an increase as the wireless services company reported that they have no plans to acquire a cable TV company. This news resulted in a rise in the company's stock value.

data.title: Fed Meeting: Key Questions As Powell Passes Torch To Warsh (Live Coverage)
data.description: There has been uncertainty surrounding Powell's future, data dependency, and balance sheet policy. As the torch is passed to Warsh following the post Fed Meeting, key questions arise. Stay updated with Live Coverage for more details on this developing situation.

data.title: Seagate Stock Soars After AI Demand Powers Big Earnings Beat
data.description: Seagate's shares surged over 15% following the data storage company's release of its fiscal Q3 earnings, which exceeded Wall Street's expectations. The robust performance was primarily driven by a significant increase in demand for artificial intelligence technology.

data.title: Thousands of attendees raise nearly $2 million for The Michael J. Fox Foundation at the 2026 Parkinson's Unity Walk
data.description: More than 3,000 individuals and families affected by Parkinson's disease gathered at Central Park in New York City on April 25. The event included a 1.2-mile walk, interactive sessions with notable speakers, and informational stalls. In total, the community managed to raise close to $2 million for the cause.

data.title: Arthur Hayes Sees Bitcoin At $125,000 By 2026 As Liquidity Drives 'Strong Uptrend'
data.description: Arthur Hayes, co-founder of BitMEX, predicts a bullish long-term trend for Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) due to favorable global liquidity conditions. The cryptocurrency is currently range-bound in the short term, but Hayes anticipates it could surge to $100,000 post-northern hemisphere summer, with a potential further increase to $125,000 by the end of 2026 if macro liquidity conditions persist. Hayes emphasizes that the primary driver of this upward trajectory is expanding dollar liquidity and global macroeconomic factors rather than regulations or political events. He notes that Bitcoin is already surpassing traditional markets like the Nasdaq and expects this trend to continue through the fall.

data.title: CN Files Shelf Prospectus Qualifying the Issuance of Debt Securities
data.description: Montreal, April 29, 2026 - CN disclosed its filing of a shelf prospectus with Canadian securities regulators and a registration statement with the US SEC. The new filings, allowing CN to issue debt securities, will be effective for 37 months, targeting both Canadian and US markets. This updated shelf prospectus and registration statement have replaced the previous ones set to expire on May 3, 2026. CN's securities are listed under TSX: CNR and NYSE: CNI.

data.title: HR Giant ADP Raises Outlook But AI Spending Faces Test
data.description: ADP (NASDAQ: ADP) released its Q3 fiscal 2026 results, reporting revenues of $5.94 billion, surpassing the expected $5.85 billion. This marks a 7% increase from the previous year. The company's adjusted EPS for the quarter was $3.37, exceeding the estimated $3.29. In terms of segment performance, Employer Services revenues were up by 7% to $4.04 billion, while PEO Services revenues saw a 7% increase to $1.91 billion. Interest on funds held for clients rose by 14% to $404 million. The average client funds balances also increased by 9% to $48.3 billion, with the average interest yield on client funds increasing by 10 basis points to 3.3%. Additionally, adjusted EBIT grew by 10% to $1.79 billion. This information can be found in full on Benzinga.com.

data.title: These Analysts Raise Their Forecasts On Bloom Energy Following Better-Than-Expected Q1 Results
data.description: Bloom Energy Corp (NYSE: BE) released strong financial results for the first quarter, surpassing expectations. The company reported revenue of $751.05 million, exceeding analyst forecasts of $551.56 million. Additionally, Bloom Energy announced adjusted earnings of 44 cents per share for the first quarter, surpassing estimates of 13 cents per share. Founder, chairman, and CEO KR Sridhar highlighted the company's role in advancing digital power solutions, positioning Bloom Energy as a leading choice for on-site power. In response to its strong performance, Bloom Energy raised its full-year 2026 revenue guidance to a new range, reflecting increased confidence in future growth.

data.title: Gedeth Research Center Reveals Global Business Resilience Amid Geopolitical Turmoil
data.description: In Wilmington, Delaware, Gedeth Network, a top consultancy firm, has published a report titled "Resilience in a Fragmented World: How Companies Are Adapting to the New Geopolitics of Trade." The study is based on data collected from over 50,000 professionals in 90 countries during Q1 of 2026. Developed in partnership with DHL Express and Universidade Europeia, the report examines the impact of the Iran-US-Israel conflict on aspects like tariffs, sales, and employment. The research delves into the shifting attitudes before and after the war, offering valuable insights for businesses navigating the changing global landscape.
BoC's Gov. Macklem: If energy prices stay higher for longer, there could well be a need to raise the policy rate.

data.title: Ty J. Young Wealth Management Celebrates 28 Years of Growth, Strategic Acquisitions, and Leadership in Retirement Planning
data.description: On April 29, 2026, Ty J. Young Wealth Management commemorated 28 years of operation since its establishment in 1998. The firm has built a strong reputation for guiding clients in retirement planning through a structured, income-oriented strategy. Over nearly three decades, the company has experienced growth and success in serving its clientele.

data.title: Visa boosts stablecoin settlement program with 5 new networks, with 50% growth in pilot program quarterly.
data.description: Visa, a major player in the payment industry, has expanded its blockchain settlement infrastructure by adding five new networks. This move comes as their pilot program reports significant growth, with a 50% increase on a quarterly basis. Visa's commitment to blockchain technology demonstrates its potential for revolutionizing the payment processing industry.
